items.hook: Ask E Jean review – Trump survivor turns out to be tougher than he thought as she fights on
items.body:

items.hook: Irving Lahrheim, known professionally as Bert Lahr, was an American actor and comedian.
items.body: He was best known for his role as the Cowardly Lion, as well as his counterpart Kansas farmworker "Zeke", in the Metro-Goldwyn-Mayer adaptation of The Wizard of Oz (1939). Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Rayleigh bath chair murder occurred in Rayleigh, Essex, England.
items.body: The Rayleigh bath chair murder was a 1943 incident of patricide in which a disabled man was killed in Rayleigh, Essex, England. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: The William Tyndale affair was a controversy in British education arising from the introduction in 1974–75 of radically progressive methods at the William Tyndale Junior School in the London Borough of Islington.
items.body: After parent protests and the publication in 1976 of a report commissioned by the Inner London Education Authority, the affair led to an increase in government authority over education in England and Wales and a reduction in the autonomy of the local education

items.hook: Kingdom of Prussia re-conquers Mainz from France.
items.body: The Kingdom of Prussia was a German state that existed from 1701 to 1918. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Ancistrus saudades is a species of freshwater ray-finned fish belonging to the family Loricariidae, the suckermouth armoured catfishes, and the subfamily Hypostominae, the suckermouth catfishes.
items.body: This catfish is foumd in northern South America. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: Jaipur is the capital and the largest city of the north-western Indian state of Rajasthan.
items.body: As of 2011, the city had a population of 3.1 million, making it the tenth most populous city in the country. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: On the Sea review – tender fishermen’s boat-based tale of forbidden love far from prying eyes
items.body:
Away from the disapproval of their Welsh fishing community, two men embark on a passionate affair in Helen Walsh’s poignant drama

items.hook: While the Mora Rebellion continues, Greeks capture Monemvasia Castle. Turkish troops and citizens are transferred to Asia Minor's coasts.
items.body: The Greek War of Independence, also known as the Greek Revolution or the Greek Revolution of 1821, was a successful war of independence fought by Greek revolutionaries against the Ottoman Empire from 1821 to 1829. One of those things you can't un-know.

items.hook: In Catalonia, Spain, the Unified Socialist Party of Catalonia is founded through the merger of Socialist and Communist parties.
items.body: Catalonia is an autonomous community of Spain, designated as a nationality by its Statute of Autonomy. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: On January 30, 1835, Andrew Jackson, the 7th president of the United States, survived an assassination attempt outside of the United States Capitol in Washington, D.C.
items.body: while returning to the White House from a funeral. Bring it up somewhere. Watch the reaction.

items.hook: The Amsden Building collapse in Framingham, Massachusetts, U.S., claimed 12 lives.
items.body: The Amsden Building collapse occurred on July 23, 1906, in Framingham, Massachusetts. The world is stranger than it lets on.

items.hook: Glenville shootout: In Cleveland, Ohio, a violent shootout between a Black Militant organization and the Cleveland Police Department occurs. During the shootout, a riot begins and lasts for five days.
items.body: The Glenville shootout was a gun battle that occurred on the night of July 23–24, 1968, in the Glenville section of Cleveland, Ohio, in the United States. Worth a quiet trip down the rabbit hole.
